title: carex iraqensis
intro: Carex iraqensis, also known as the Iraqi sedge, is a type of perennial plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wetlands and marshy areas. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex iraqensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems. Carex iraqensis can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, to harness its medicinal properties.
